html:
Exclusive to:  

Wobster Dinner is a Crock Pot recipe introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. It is cooked by adding 1 Wobster and 1 Butter. It takes 20 seconds to cook. It restores 60 , 37.5 , and 50 , making it one of the best food items to restore both Health and Sanity.

Adding any Meats to the recipe will produce Meatballs instead. Adding any additional Fish with meat value will result in Seafood Gumbo instead, unless a Twig is also added, which may result in Fishsticks. Adding Ice will result in Wobster Bisque instead. Adding Shark Fin will result in Shark Fin Soup instead.

This recipe has a higher Crock Pot Priority than Monster Lasagna, therefore it allows using more than one Durians and/or Jellyfishes as fillers. However, Monster Meat cannot be used due to recipe restriction of not allowing meats.

Prerequisites

  • Requires:
  • Requires:
  • Excludes:
  • Excludes:

Recipe

×2

Fillers cannot be Meats, Fishes (with meat value) or Ice

Example

×2

 A recipe for Wobster Dinner that extenuates hunger value

×2

×2

Exchange

Tips

  • Wobster Dinner is a good food choice if the player has a butterfly farm and a reliable source of Wobsters, thanks to its high Health and Sanity benefits.
  • If Sanity is not an issue, it may be a better idea to instead use the Butter to make Waffles. Waffles heal the same amount of Health and Hunger (unless you are playing as Maxwell) and uses ingredients that are much more abundant than Wobsters.
  • Warly's quote for Lobster Dinner in DST has been changed to "No place is too remote for some fine dining!", likely due to his original quote to Wobster Dinner referencing the setting of Shipwrecked.

'''Wobster Dinner''' is a [[Crock Pot]] recipe introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. It is cooked by adding 1 [[Wobster]] and 1 [[Butter]]. It takes 20 seconds to cook. It restores 60 {{Hp}}, 37.5 {{Hunger}}, and 50 {{Sanity}}, making it one of the best food items to restore both [[Health]] and [[Sanity]].

Adding any Meats to the recipe will produce [[Meatballs]] instead. Adding any additional [[Fishes|Fish]] with meat value will result in [[Seafood Gumbo]] instead, unless a [[Twigs|Twig]] is also added, which may result in [[Fishsticks]]. Adding Ice will result in [[Lobster Bisque|Wobster Bisque]] instead. Adding [[Shark Fin]] will result in [[Shark Fin Soup]] instead.

This recipe has a higher [[Crock Pot#Priority|Crock Pot Priority]] than Monster Lasagna, therefore it allows using more than one [[Durian]]s and/or [[Jellyfish]]es as fillers. However, Monster Meat cannot be used due to recipe restriction of not allowing meats.
==Prerequisites==
* Requires: [[File:Wobster.png|link=Wobster]]
*Requires: [[File:Butter.png|link=Butter]]
*Excludes: [[File:Ices.png|link=Ice]]
*Excludes: [[File:Meats.png|link=Meats]]

== {{pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Wobster
|item2 = Butter
|item3 = Filler
|count3 = 2
|tool = Crock Pot
|result =Wobster Dinner
|note = Fillers cannot be [[Meats]], [[Fishes|Fishes (with meat value)]] or [[Ice]]
}}

}}

== {{Pic32|Dubloons}} Exchange ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Wobster Dinner
|tool = Yaarctopus Icon
|result = Boat Cannon
}}

== {{Pic32|Prototype}}Tips ==

* Wobster Dinner is a good food choice if the player has a butterfly farm and a reliable source of Wobsters, thanks to its high [[Health]] and [[Sanity]] benefits.
* If Sanity is not an issue, it may be a better idea to instead use the Butter to make [[Waffles]]. Waffles heal the same amount of Health and Hunger (unless you are playing as Maxwell) and uses ingredients that are much more abundant than Wobsters.
* [[Warly]]'s quote for Lobster Dinner in DST has been changed to ''"No place is too remote for some fine dining!"'', likely due to his original quote to Wobster Dinner referencing the setting of ''Shipwrecked''.
